signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Volatility + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 162.14, holding above the critical 160.00 threshold where BoJ verbal and physical intervention risks escalate. The 264bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage continues to support the USD bid, while JPY speculative positioning remains heavily short with COT LF net short at -115,033 contracts. However, the options market is actively hedging downside risk, with USD/JPY 25d risk reversals showing a moderate put skew of -1.08 and a significant volatility risk premium of +5.0% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.2%). This options skew contradicts the spot trend, signaling institutional hedging against sudden JPY appreciation. Carry remains the dominant driver under the current normal volatility regime (VIX at 17.6), but a break above 162.50 or a spike in VIX above 25 will trigger rapid carry unwinds.

signals.title: EUR/AUD — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBA Minutes
signals.text: The G10 Currency Strength Scorecard triggers a bilateral theme warning with a 0.971% spread between leader EUR (+0.414% avg) and laggard AUD (-0.556% avg). This divergence is driven by the RBA June Minutes, which revealed policymakers opted to leave rates on hold and chose patience over a hawkish policy pivot, disappointing markets positioned for an immediate hike. This policy stance clashes with the -60bp carry disadvantage for EUR/USD (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), showing that policy momentum is overriding nominal carry. EUR/AUD strength is further supported by extreme retail positioning, with EUR/AUD retail sentiment at 67% short, providing contrarian fuel for further upside. Watch the 1.6700 level on EUR/AUD to confirm a structural breakout.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Sentiment Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD trades at 0.5646, exhibiting an extreme divergence between institutional and retail positioning. COT LF speculative positioning is net short -23,314 contracts, while retail sentiment is at an extreme 94% long. This represents a major contrarian reversal risk as retail traders attempt to fade the institutional downtrend. The downward pressure is fundamentally supported by a +150bp carry advantage favoring the USD (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) and a deeply negative NZD real policy rate of -0.83% (inflation expectations at 3.08% vs policy rate of 2.25%). Retail capitulation risk remains high; a break below 0.5600 will accelerate the sell-off. Watch the RBNZ policy meeting on July 8 for any hawkish pivot to invalidate the short bias.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Volatility Complacency
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4229, showing a significant cross-asset correlation break against WTI crude oil. The current correlation stands at -0.761 vs a 252-day norm of +0.020 (z = -2.11σ), indicating that the traditional CAD-commodity link has broken down as the pair is driven entirely by the +150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage and a positive US Economic Surprise Index (CESI net +290 beats vs CAD net -6 misses). Speculative positioning is heavily short CAD (COT LF net short -87,096), while retail sentiment is at an extreme 94% short. Despite the strong upward trend, the options market shows mild call skew (+0.37) and lacks a significant volatility premium, suggesting complacency. Watch for a reversal if WTI oil breaks above $72.0 or if US jobs data misses expectations.
evidence: USD/CAD vs WTI Oil corr: -0.761 (norm: +0.020, z: -2.11σ)
